title: Samantha API — Architecture, Tooling & Standards
topic: Samantha API — Architecture, Capability & Tooling Research
summary: Architecture decisions, tooling landscape, and wire standards for the Samantha external developer API.
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Overview
  2. 2. Key Findings
  3. 3. API Architecture Comparison
  4. 4. Current State — What's Built
  5. 5. Tooling Recommendations
  6. 6. Standards Reference
  7. 7. Gaps & Next Steps
  8. 8. Sources

1. Overview

Samantha is the developer-facing API and SDK surface for Vector. It operates at two layers simultaneously: an internal React SDK (context-based hooks and providers for custom apps mounted inside Vector) and the foundation of a public REST API for external developers, CI pipelines, and third-party integrations. The internal SDK layer is partially built; the external REST API layer (API keys, URL versioning, public docs) does not yet exist.

This paper captures the architecture decision, a comparison of API paradigm options, the tooling landscape for management and SDK generation, and the wire-level standards we should adopt (key format, error bodies, pagination, versioning).

2. Key Findings

  • REST + SSE is the correct hybrid. REST for all CRUD and queries; Server-Sent Events (SSE) for streaming (AI output, job progress, live feeds). WebSocket stays reserved for bidirectional real-time features only (e.g. live collaboration canvas). gRPC only if Go microservice split ever occurs.
  • No OpenAPI spec exists today. All API docs are hand-written MDX in a Docusaurus site under api-reference/. This is the single biggest gap — the spec unlocks Scalar docs, SDK generation, and contract linting.
  • No external API key auth. Only JWT Bearer tokens today. External developer access requires a separate key management layer.
  • No URL versioning. No /v1/ prefix exists on any route. Breaking changes are uncontrolled until this lands.
  • Samantha SDK root is a stub. app/lib/samantha.ts is empty. The portfolio.fields.* surface is fully contracted (docs/c_samantha_sdk_fields.md) but not yet runtime-bound.
  • OpenAPI (the spec format) is not dying. Version 3.1 (2021) aligned with JSON Schema; 3.2 is in active development. The tooling ecosystem (Scalar, Redocly, Speakeasy) is growing.
  • Scalar is the right doc portal. MIT licensed, self-hostable, best in-class "Try It" experience. Feeds directly from an OpenAPI spec.
  • Speakeasy is the right SDK generator when quality matters — idiomatic Go/Python/TypeScript output, air-gap capable standalone binary, native Go support fits the stack.
  • Roll-your-own API key management (~300 lines Go + Postgres) is the correct starting point. Unkey (Go-native, self-hostable) is the upgrade path if lifecycle features are needed fast.

3. API Architecture Comparison

ParadigmBest ForStrengthsWeaknessesVerdict
RESTPublic developer APIs, third-party integrationsUniversal client support; HTTP caching; mature OpenAPI ecosystem; horizontal scaling trivialOver/under-fetching without care; no built-in push✓ Primary paradigm
SSEServer-to-client streaming — LLM tokens, job progress, live feedsPlain HTTP GET; works through any proxy/CDN; browser auto-reconnect via Last-Event-ID; auth via standard Bearer headerUnidirectional only; some reverse proxies buffer by default✓ Add for streaming
WebSocketBidirectional real-time — collaborative editing, live cursorsFull duplex; low latency both directionsStateful; breaks horizontal scaling without sticky sessions/broker; complex auth (no custom WS headers in browser)Reserve for canvas only
GraphQLInternal product data layer with highly variable client requirementsSingle endpoint; clients request exact fields; typed schemaHTTP caching impossible without persisted queries; N+1 moves to server; poor external developer storyInternal only, if at all
tRPCTypeScript monorepos, same team owns FE + BEEnd-to-end type inference; zero schema duplication; no codegenTypeScript/Node backend only — does not apply to Go; no external developer story; no OpenAPI outputNot applicable (Go backend)
gRPCService-to-service internal trafficBinary Protobuf (60–80% bandwidth reduction); HTTP/2 native; bidirectional streamingCannot be called from browser without gRPC-Web + Envoy; steep toolchain; poor external DXFuture only, if microservice split

4. Current State — What's Built

The backend is Go + chi router. The architecture follows a strict boundary-enforced pattern:

request → chi.Router
         → auth middleware (JWT validation + RBAC permission resolution)
         → handler (thin translation layer)
         → service (business logic + sole writer per critical table)
         → database (Postgres, tenant-isolated via subscription_id)

Authentication & Authorization

  • JWT Bearer tokens — Authorization: Bearer <token> for all standard routes
  • WebSocket — accepts ?access_token= query param (browsers can't send custom WS headers)
  • Custom headers — X-CI-Token for build-reconcile; X-Custom-App-Token for production custom-app registration; X-CSRF-Token for mutation protection
  • RBAC — 5 seeded system roles, 26 permissions; process-local resolver with 60s TTL cache (PLA-0007)

Frozen SDK Sub-Surfaces

Sub-surfaceAddressStatusDoc
samantha._viewport.*Addressables registryFrozen (PLA-0005)docs/c_c_addressables.md
samantha.diagram.canvasCanvas2D + dagre + d3-zoomFrozen v1.0.0 (PLA-0006)docs/c_c_diagram_canvas.md
samantha.portfolio.fields.*Custom field read/write SDKContracted, not runtime-bounddocs/c_samantha_sdk_fields.md

Address Format (Frozen)

samantha._viewport.<slot>._<kind>.<name>[._<kind>.<name>…]

Slots: app | header | footer | side_bar | modal | toast. Name validation: /^[a-z0-9_]{1,64}$/. Leading underscore on every system segment; user names never have underscores.

5. Tooling Recommendations

API Documentation

ToolModelFitCostVerdict
ScalarOpenAPI → hosted/self-hostedBest "Try It" DX; MIT license; clean UI; embeddableFree / $24/mo Pro✓ Recommended
MintlifyMDX + OpenAPI → docs siteFull docs site (guides + reference); git-native; generates llms.txt for LLM discoverability$149/mo+If full doc site needed
RedoclyOpenAPI + MarkdownEnterprise governance, CLI linting, style guidesCustomOverkill for small team
Swagger UIOpenAPI → embeddedZero cost, universally understoodFreeDated; avoid for external docs
Readme.ioManaged developer hubAnalytics, user-personalized docs, changelogs, forum$399/mo+Full DevRel; expensive

SDK Generation

ToolAir-gapLanguagesQualityCostVerdict
SpeakeasyYes (standalone binary)10 incl. Go, Python, TS, JavaHigh — idiomatic output, Zod runtime validation (TS), 1 dep in TSPaid (tiered)✓ Production SDK gen
OpenAPI GeneratorYes50+Inconsistent — Java roots, ~4,500 open issuesFreeBootstrap / spec validation only
StainlessNo (vendor-hosted)7High$250/mo per SDKQuality comparable, more expensive
FernNo (vendor-hosted)7High — acquired by Postman Jan 2026, future uncertain$250/mo per SDKAvoid — strategic uncertainty

API Key Management

OptionStackSelf-hostVerdict
Roll-your-ownGo + PostgresYes✓ Start here — ~300 lines, full control
UnkeyGo + MySQL + RedisYesUpgrade path if lifecycle features needed fast (rebuilt Dec 2025)
FusioPHPYesAvoid — stack mismatch

API Gateway

ToolStackVerdict
Application middlewareGo✓ Start here — no operational overhead
KrakenDGo, statelessAdd when separate management plane needed; no DB dependency
TykGo + RedisIf integrated developer portal + analytics required
Kong / APISIXLua/NGINXAvoid — Postgres/etcd overhead not warranted

6. Standards Reference

API Key Format

sam_live_<32-char-base62>   # production
sam_test_<32-char-base62>   # sandbox/test
  • Store only SHA-256 hash; return raw key exactly once at creation
  • Header: Authorization: Bearer <key> (not X-API-Key — better OAuth tooling compat)
  • Prefix is human-readable in logs and detectable by secret scanning tools (GitHub, truffleHog)
  • Never accept keys in URL query parameters — they appear in server, CDN, and Referer logs
  • Minimum entropy: 128 bits (22+ chars of base62)

Rate Limiting Headers

X-RateLimit-Limit: 1000              # legacy compat (GitHub/Stripe model)
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 950
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1746662400        # Unix timestamp
RateLimit: r=950;t=1847              # IETF draft (forward-compat)

Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After: <seconds> (RFC 9110).

Error Format — RFC 9457 Problem Details

Content-Type: application/problem+json

{
  "type": "https://api.vector.dev/errors/validation-failed",
  "title": "Validation Failed",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "One or more fields failed validation.",
  "instance": "/v1/artefacts/defects/abc123",
  "violations": [
    { "field": "name", "message": "required" }
  ]
}
  • type is a stable URI that identifies the problem class (should dereference to docs)
  • title is invariant across occurrences of the same problem type
  • instance identifies this specific occurrence — useful for log correlation and support
  • Extension fields allowed (e.g. violations, retryAfter)

Pagination — Cursor-Based

GET /v1/portfolios?limit=50&after=cur_01HV3X...

Response:
{
  "data": [...],
  "pagination": {
    "hasNextPage": true,
    "hasPrevPage": false,
    "nextCursor": "cur_01HV3X...",
    "prevCursor": null
  }
}

Link: <https://api.vector.dev/v1/portfolios?limit=50&after=cur_01HV3X...>; rel="next"
  • Cursor is opaque base64 encoding (created_at, id) — never expose raw DB offsets
  • Avoids OFFSET N full table scans; consistent when data changes mid-pagination
  • Avoid exact counts on large tables — use hasMore: true or cap with "totalCountCapped": true

Versioning

  • Primary: /v1/ URL path prefix on all external Samantha API routes — explicit, cacheable, routable in Go path middleware
  • Secondary: Samantha-Version: 2026-05-06 header for date-pinning within a major version (Stripe-style) — allows additive changes within v1 without a full version bump
  • Support previous major version 12 months post-deprecation announcement
  • Never silently break a versioned endpoint — breaking changes require a new version number

7. Gaps & Next Steps

GapPriorityUnlocks
OpenAPI 3.1 specHighScalar docs, SDK gen, contract linting
External API key authHighThird-party developer access
URL versioning (/v1/)HighControlled breaking changes
RFC 9457 error formatMediumConsistent error handling across all clients
Rate limiting headersMediumDeveloper visibility into quota usage
Cursor-based paginationMediumScale + consistency on large datasets
SSE streaming endpointsMediumAI output streaming, job progress
samantha.portfolio.fields.* runtime bindingHighPhase 1 custom field SDK for custom apps
Developer portal UI (key management)LowSelf-serve external developer onboarding
